AnnualCrop.description: Agricultural land dedicated to crops that are planted and harvested within a single growing season, such as wheat, corn, or rice.

Forest.description: Dense woodland areas with significant tree canopy coverage, playing a crucial role in carbon sequestration and biodiversity.

HerbaceousVegetation.description: Areas covered with non-woody plants such as grasses, herbs, and ferns. Common in meadows and natural grasslands.

Highway.description: Major road infrastructure including highways, motorways, and large transportation corridors connecting urban areas.

Industrial.description: Zones designated for industrial activity including factories, warehouses, commercial facilities, and logistics centers.

Pasture.description: Grasslands used for grazing livestock such as cattle and sheep. Managed agricultural land with maintained grass cover.

PermanentCrop.description: Land with long-term crops that don't need replanting annually — including vineyards, orchards, olive groves, and fruit plantations.

Residential.description: Urban and suburban areas with housing, apartment buildings, and associated community infrastructure.

River.description: Natural waterways and river systems including banks and surrounding riparian zones essential for freshwater ecosystems.

SeaLake.description: Large bodies of water including seas, lakes, and reservoirs. Important for water supply, fisheries, and ecosystem balance.
